mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, lower discount rates raise the present value of future equity cash flows and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is declining than when it is flat or rising, or if the sign of the rolling 1-year correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX returns is consistently negative, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE rise above recent levels signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, which depresses equity earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return in triggered windows is not below the unconditional mean by at least one standard error.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, consumer spending capacity and realized retail demand strengthen simultaneously, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through higher sales expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage across cyclical firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO monthly shocks are followed by negative or statistically indistinguishable 20-day SPX returns over a persistent rolling sample, or if the hit rate falls below 50% after accounting for revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates become more negative and financial conditions ease, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by a widening margin
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-minus-policy-rate condition does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and tightening wealth effects, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as cyclicals and consumer demand soften.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly declines in HOUST are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the predictive relationship flips positive for more than one year in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a softening labor market, rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS growth identifies a broader demand recession, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth turns negative over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration signal does not improve prediction versus either series alone, or if triggered episodes still yield positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate transmits through higher discount rates and tighter liquidity, so the same rate increase should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when the policy rate is above neutral-like levels.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive stance
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS increases above 4.5% are not followed by weaker 20-day SPX returns than comparable low-rate periods, or if the conditional rank IC is not negative in tightening regimes.
